parts: Name this city which contains buildings like Tornado Tower and the 300-meter-tall Aspire Tower. The planned Lusail Iconic Stadium will be built north of this city.

bonuses.leadin: One project leader was inspired by the design of this work to create a line of prefabricated low-income housing. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this film series. Kanye West was later forced to shut down building the aforementioned dome-like houses, inspired by Tunisian desert housing which was used to represent Tatooine (“TAH-too-een”) in this film series.

The Jedi Academy Library was ripped off from the Long Room in the library of this country’s oldest college. Medieval monasteries located in this country include Clonmacnoise (“CLON-muck-NEE-shuh”) and Skellig Michael, where the Ahch-To (“OCK-TOE”) scenes in The Last Jedi were filmed.
This architect designed the UAE's RAK Convention Center, which looks suspiciously like the Death Star, as well as Casa de Musica, which looks like a Jawa sandcrawler. This man also wrote S, M, L, XL.

bonuses.leadin: One house designed by this person has a so-called ‘disappearing corner’ and was constructed for a woman whose ideas on that house meant that she was given a co-designer credit. For 10 points each:
parts: Bart van der Leck told this designer to make a chair he had more colorful. This designer had that chair inscribed with the poem Der Aesthet by Christian Morgenstern.
Gerrit Rietveld was from this country. He designed the Metz & Co building in the Hague, which is located in this country.
This later Dutch designer and architect designed the Seattle Central Library and wrote the book Delirious New York, which praises the ‘chance-like’ nature of city living.

bonuses.leadin: The cover of a book by this architect features his wife Madelon Vriesendorp’s (VREE-zen-dorp's) painting Flagrant Delit, which shows two distorted skyscrapers lying in bed next to the Statue of Liberty’s torch. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this architect who wrote Delirious New York. With Ole Scheeren (OH-luh SHAY-ren), this architect designed Beijing’s CCTV headquarters, which features a 75-meter perpendicular cantilevered section suspended from two separate towers.
Koolhaas designed the glass-and-steel central library in this American city. The 1962 World’s Fair in this city produced its Pacific Science Center and Space Needle.

Koolhaas’s 2002 never-adopted redesign of the flag of the European Union is typically referred to by its similarity to this visual pattern. The flag has been praised for being easily updated to reflect the entry of new member states.

Rem Koolhaas’s OMA recently created a series of huge art galleries for this Milan-headquartered company, as well as a weird multipurpose building for it called a “Transformer” in Seoul. This company, whose subsidiaries include Miu Miu, commissioned Herzog & De Meuron to create an all-glass store in Tokyo.
In 2006, OMA also worked on the pavilion for the Serpentine Galleries in this city, which is.also home to the Tate Modern art gallery and Big Ben.
